Bug / Task Description: The death-clock backend reconstructs remaining life expectancy from an SSA q(x) mortality curve in app/model/baseline.py via life_expectancy_from_qx(qx_curve, start_age). The current implementation sums the start-of-year survivor counts, which overstates life expectancy because it credits a full year of life even to people who die during the year. Implement the standard actuarial method: for each year, the people who die should be credited only a partial (half) year of life, i.e. accumulate the mid-period survivor count L(x) = l(x) - 0.5*d(x) rather than l(x), where d(x) = l(x)*q(x). Probabilities must be capped at 1.0. The reconstructed value must closely track the published SSA e(x) table (get_baseline_life_expectancy) and a single fully-fatal year (q(x)=1) must yield 0.5 remaining years, not 1.0. Keep life expectancy strictly decreasing with starting age.
Commit Subject: actuarial life expectancy with mid-period (L(x)) correction
